message:
feat(resolv): port res_gethostby* aliases + res_send hooks + ns_name_ntol/rollback (bd-x8z63)

Seven more libresolv entries:

  - res_gethostbyname / res_gethostbyname2 / res_gethostbyaddr —
    deprecated res_-prefixed front-ends that thinly wrap our existing
    gethostbyname / gethostbyname2 / gethostbyaddr. Provided so binaries
    that link against the historical BIND symbol resolve cleanly.
  - res_send_setqhook / res_send_setrhook — install pre-query and
    post-response callback hooks. Stored per-thread (we have no
    pluggable resolver core to invoke them); a doc-hidden
    res_send_qhook_for_tests / rhook_for_tests accessor exposes the
    stored values for round-trip verification.
  - ns_name_ntol(src, dst, dstsiz) — convert a wire-format DNS name
    (length-prefixed labels terminated by a 0 byte) to lowercase. Caps
    traversal at 256 bytes. Returns -1 on dst overflow or malformed
    label; tolerates a single compression pointer (top two bits set).
  - ns_name_rollback(srcp, dnptrs, lastdnptr) — walk dnptrs[] forward
    and clear the first slot whose pointer is at or past srcp, used to
    undo partial name-compression entries on error paths.

Tests: 11 new abi tests covering numeric IPv4 routing through both
res_ and un-prefixed forms, hook round-trip via thread-local store,
case-lowering of mixed labels, root-name (single 0 byte) handling,
rejection of dst-too-small, and rollback threshold semantics with a
synthetic dnptrs array backed by a real buffer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
